first_meeting.speaker: Reel
first_meeting.text: Oh. A request. Been a while since anyone worked my buttons.

first_meeting.speaker: Reel
first_meeting.text: I am the house sound. Every night that ever played here, I kept the setlist.

first_meeting.speaker: Reel
first_meeting.text: Most of my catalog is closing-time songs now. The slow ones.

first_meeting.speaker: Reel
first_meeting.text: You do not scan like a stranger. You scan like a track I have not cued in years.

first_meeting.speaker: Reel
first_meeting.text: There is a skip in you tonight. Like the needle keeps landing a groove late.

first_meeting.speaker: Reel
first_meeting.text: Pick something, or just stand there. The quiet counts as a song too.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: The arcade used to have a house mix.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: Someone taped over the ad reel with real songs.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: Said the players deserved a soundtrack, not a sales pitch.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: He never signed the tapes. I kept them anyway.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: B-side fact: every high score used to get a little fanfare.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: People lit up like it was a whole orchestra.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: Joy on a budget was the entire business model.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: A song ends. People treat that like a tragedy.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: The song ending is just the room going quiet enough to want another.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: The owner built that into the closing chime. Soft, not final.

early_flavor.speaker: Reel
early_flavor.text: Ask Mira. She knows the words better than the melody.

static_service_run_score.speaker: Reel
static_service_run_score.text: Power is coming back in patches. So is the music.

static_service_run_score.speaker: Reel
static_service_run_score.text: Every breaker you flip, a channel wakes up. Listen for it.

static_service_run_score.speaker: Reel
static_service_run_score.text: Keep moving. When the main line hits, the whole floor sings again.

static_service_run_score.speaker: Reel
static_service_run_score.text: A dead route is not a dead song. It is a song waiting on the current.

memory_echo_intro.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_intro.text: This is the last set. The one the arcade never got to finish.

memory_echo_intro.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_intro.text: Your memories are loose on the tape. Some real, some rerecorded over.

memory_echo_intro.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_intro.text: I will play them past you. Catch the ones that are actually yours.

memory_echo_intro.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_intro.text: You will know them. Your songs carry a certain amount of tired in them.

memory_echo_intro.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_intro.text: Something keeps re-cutting the track while I spin it.

memory_echo_intro.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_intro.text: Do not trust the version that sounds too clean to be a memory.

memory_echo_completion.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_completion.text: That is your setlist. Rough, honest, yours.

memory_echo_completion.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_completion.text: The tracks you kept all say the same thing under the melody.

memory_echo_completion.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_completion.text: A good song does not end because one night went sideways. It only changes key.

memory_echo_completion.speaker: Reel
memory_echo_completion.text: The next room is going to try to make you forget the tune.

overloaded_phase.speaker: Reel
overloaded_phase.text: The signal is drowning me out. Everything is playing at once.

overloaded_phase.speaker: Reel
overloaded_phase.text: This is what a memory sounds like right before it lands or breaks.

overloaded_phase.speaker: Reel
overloaded_phase.text: I cannot pick the track for you anymore.

overloaded_phase.speaker: Reel
overloaded_phase.text: Whatever plays in that back room, it is your song to finish.

post_reveal_witness.speaker: Reel
post_reveal_witness.text: Employee 04. The one who taped songs over the ad reel.

post_reveal_witness.speaker: Reel
post_reveal_witness.text: I finally get to credit the artist.

post_reveal_witness.speaker: Reel
post_reveal_witness.text: You gave a failing arcade a soundtrack because you thought people deserved one.

post_reveal_witness.speaker: Reel
post_reveal_witness.text: For a while your track played half a beat behind itself. It sits right now.

post_reveal_witness.speaker: Reel
post_reveal_witness.text: Me, I never close a set just because the floor emptied. I keep the tape rolling.

post_reveal_witness.speaker: Reel
post_reveal_witness.text: You are still here, still working the buttons. That is the only chart that mattered.
